title: Implement PROP-070 — Step A0c assigned-analyst chain-aware close sweep + Step 8 self-test + tinker observability counter; tinker has self-apply empowerment per PROP-070's rollout_plan step 2
background:
PROP-070 (authored by tinker 2026-05-31T15:13Z in response to DIRECTIVE-009) corrected the operator's framing about the burndown gap and identified two distinct bugs:

  Gap A — DIRECT (6 stuck ISSs, 2 confirmed bypass + 4 EXPs-not-yet-integrated): Step 8 (canonical close-on-EXP-integration in decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md) is sometimes bypassed by the decider LLM. Commit 1727ee3 integrated EXP-464 and EXP-465 (marked integrated=true in tracker) but did NOT close ISS-2326 or ISS-2327 (which are in those EXPs' issue_ids). This is a LLM-skip-by-omission failure mode similar to PROP-066/PROP-068's class.

  Gap B — CONSOLIDATED (12 stuck ISSs): the existing end-of-run sweeps (PROP-056 Step A0 for status='closed' residue, PROP-058 Step A0b for status='blocked-on-curmudgeon' residue) do NOT handle status='assigned-analyst' ISSs whose EXP-chain endpoint has reached integrated=true. The chain awareness is already built correctly into the issue_ids aggregation at consolidation time; neither sweep targets the right status.

Operator has reviewed PROP-070 and approved the proposed_fix. Tinker is granted Mode 4 self-apply empowerment to deploy the three files_to_modify items in this run, per PROP-070's rollout_plan step 2.

This directive is sequenced ahead of a planned decider-cadence bump from 4h to 2h. The bump will exercise Step 8 more frequently; shipping PROP-070's Step 8 self-test BEFORE the bump prevents accumulating new Step-8-bypass stuck ISSs during the higher-cadence period.

amendment_noted_heuristic.operator_choice: Accept tinker's proposed hold-back rule as designed: do NOT auto-close ISSs whose chain endpoint has integration_mode starting with 'amendment-noted-'. Amendment EXPs are conditional on their parent EXP's integration; if parent isn't integrated yet, the amendment-noted EXP's integration alone is insufficient signal.
amendment_noted_heuristic.rationale: The 3 stuck ISSs (ISS-2343/2344/2345 from today's burndown that were correctly NOT closed) are exactly this pattern — they point to amendment EXPs (EXP-476/478) that exist as files but await EXP-450 integration. The hold-back protects them from premature closure.

step_8_self_test_fail_loud.operator_choice: FAIL LOUD as designed. Step 8 self-test should abort the decider commit if any EXP marked integrated=true in this run still has unmodified issue_ids in open-issues.json.
step_8_self_test_fail_loud.rationale: The bypass is a real bug class (same shape as PROP-066/068 skip-by-omission). Aborting the commit on detection gives us the strongest possible signal — operator-visible failure rather than silent leak that surfaces days later. Decider runs are recoverable; an aborted run gets re-tried on next cadence without data loss.

requested_work: Step 1 — Apply files_to_modify item 1: monitor/prompts/decider.md. Insert Step A0c sweep AFTER PROP-058 Step A0b's closing block + 'Why a sweep' paragraph, BEFORE '## End-of-Run Step A: Analyst Attention Inbox'. Mirror PROP-058 A0b's code structure per PROP-070's approximate_code_pattern. Include the 48h recently-touched guard, chain walker (max depth 8), amendment-noted hold-back, closure_evidence with chain path, closure_by_mechanism='step-a0c-sweep'. Add the self-test that fail-loud-aborts if any remaining assigned-analyst entry doesn't satisfy the safety predicates.

Step 2 — Apply files_to_modify item 2: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md. Append Step 8 self-test paragraph IMMEDIATELY AFTER Step 8 ('Close related issues — do NOT skip.') and BEFORE Step 8b. Self-test reads open-issues + tracker + archive, finds the just-integrated EXP, computes leaked = exp.issue_ids.filter(id => oi.issues.find(i => i.id===id && i.status !== 'fixed')), and FAILS LOUD + aborts commit if leaked.length > 0. Mirrors the operator's fail-loud decision per PROP-070 Q-OP on Step 8.

Step 3 — Apply files_to_modify item 3: monitor/prompts/tinker.md. Add a Mode 2 quick-check observability counter for assigned-analyst ISSs whose EXP-chain endpoint is integrated. Print as 'PROP-070 observability: N orphan-integrated assigned-analyst ISSs' in the run report. Expected to be 0 after PROP-070 deploys; >0 immediately means there are pre-existing cases the sweep didn't catch (escalate for investigation).

Step 4 — Validate per PROP-070's verification_pattern: `grep -q 'Step A0c: assigned-analyst Chain-Aware Close Sweep' monitor/prompts/decider.md && grep -q 'Step 8 self-test (PROP-070' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md && grep -q 'PROP-070 observability' monitor/prompts/tinker.md && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN`. Expected output: FIXED.

Step 5 — Commit + push the 3 file changes in ONE batched commit (operator preference: atomic landing for inter-related prompt edits). Commit message should reference DIRECTIVE-011 + PROP-070 + the two gap bug class identifiers.

Step 6 — Manually copy the 3 prompt files to FUSE (workspace path) so the next scheduled cycle of decider/tinker picks up the new logic. Per-session FUSE staleness pattern documented in D31-010 (FND-01 + baby tracker observations); manual sync is the bootstrap path until PROP-071 lands a systematic fix.

Step 7 — Lifecycle: flip PROP-070.json status='implemented' with implementation_commit + implementation_at + implementation_run recorded per PROP-070's rollout_plan step 6. Flip this directive to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-070'.

Step 8 — Post-deployment canary plan documented in run report: (a) at next decider run (every 4h pre-bump, every 2h post-bump per directive title), verify the run report includes a 'Step A0c sweep' line showing migrated count + held-open count + endpoint chain paths; (b) at next tinker Mode 2 run, verify PROP-070 observability counter prints (expect 0); (c) if either signal absent or counter >0 after 1-2 cycles, file follow-up directive for investigation.
non_goals: Do NOT also bump decider cadence in this run — that's a SEPARATE operator action after PROP-070 ships (per directive title preamble). Operator will use scheduled-tasks tool to update dome-decider cron from '0 2,6,10,14,18,22 * * *' (every 4h) to '0 */2 * * *' (every 2h) post-deployment.

Do NOT re-close the 18 ISSs from today's operator-burndown — they are correctly closed (16 prematurely-but-defensibly, 2 genuinely-stuck). The Step A0c sweep + Step 8 self-test prevent FUTURE occurrences of the same bug class; today's burndown handled the existing residue.

Do NOT amend the burndown closure entries' closure_reason fields to reflect tinker's framing correction — the audit_grandfathered=yes signals 'bookkeeping' already; the slight inaccuracy in the closure_reason text is not load-bearing.
Do NOT touch PROP-068's Step 4 collapse architecture — PROP-070 is a DIFFERENT bug class (consumer-side closure logic vs producer-side cleanup execution). Independent fixes.
Do NOT extend scope to include the amendment-noted heuristic implementation outside what PROP-070 prescribed — operator approved the proposed hold-back; do NOT add additional special-case logic.

context_pointers.operator_burndown_commit: 7ec29efbbd at 2026-05-31T13:00:00Z — closed 18 stuck ISSs; the residue PROP-070 prevents from recurring
context_pointers.step_8_canonical_location: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md — Step 8 'Close related issues — do NOT skip'
context_pointers.prop_058_step_a0b_pattern: monitor/prompts/decider.md — Step A0b is the pattern Step A0c mirrors
context_pointers.amendment_pattern_examples_preserved: ISS-2343/2344/2345 (still open, awaiting EXP-450 + EXP-476/478 amendment integration) — these MUST NOT be closed by Step A0c due to amendment-noted hold-back rule. Test against these specifically in the sweep's dry-run output.
context_pointers.bypass_evidence_commit: 1727ee3 (decider-2026-05-31T12-28) — integrated EXP-464/465 but didn't close ISS-2326/2327; this is the canonical Step 8 bypass case

expected_output: Single commit on origin/main containing: monitor/prompts/decider.md (Step A0c added), monitor/prompts/reference/decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md (Step 8 self-test added), monitor/prompts/tinker.md (PROP-070 observability counter added). PLUS: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-070-*.json status flipped to 'implemented' + implementation_commit recorded. PLUS: this directive flipped to status='completed' with prop_id_implemented='PROP-070'. PLUS: FUSE-bootstrap-synced for the 3 prompt files.
estimated_effort: ~30-45 min Opus per PROP-070's estimated_impact. The pattern-mirroring (Step A0b → A0c) is mechanical; the self-test is precise but small.
priority_note: MEDIUM — sequenced BEFORE the planned decider-cadence bump (operator post-deploy action). Shipping PROP-070's Step 8 self-test before bumping cadence prevents accumulating new Step-8-bypass stuck ISSs during the higher-cadence period. The bypass-detection is fail-loud, so any post-bump regression will be immediately operator-visible.
